parsed_review.summary: CP00-670 is a descriptor-only Risk-B service-implementation-slice pack (40 units, RP22.P02.M03.S01-RP22.P02.M04.S18; deliverable distribution contract:2/implementation:18/security_audit:4/ui:6/claude_review:2/failure_recovery:4/test:4; micro Primary:22/Secondary:18) continuing RP22 External Integrations I with all runtimes closed. Static read-only inspection confirms the pack-level posture is honest and well-supported: manifest, command-evidence, construction-inspection, and claude-review-result all hold production_ready=false and runtime_ready=false (no runtime/production/enterprise-trust overclaim), and descriptor/validators/tests/contracts consistently enforce descriptor_only, runtime_execution=false, persistence_boundary_no_write, implementation_runtime_opened=false, no_real_data, customer-safe errors, no raw payloads, and every provider/OAuth/sync/message/e-sign/webhook/credential/permission/audit/UI/Hermes runtime closed. validators.js validateExternalIntegrationsICp670ServiceImplementationSliceDescriptor and the model.test.js CP670 tests assert these boundaries plus coverage, and validate-rp22-external-integrations-i-contract.mjs regenerates a projection that matches both committed contract files (generated_from_pack_id/current_pack=CP00-670, correct descriptor type). Hermes is used only for deterministic review packet/receipt handling (hermes_runtime_opened=false, runtime_receipt_emitted=false); Claude stays a read-only reviewer (claude_is_final_approval=false, promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false) and not final approval authority, with the human/enterprise-trust approval boundary correctly outside this receipt. No secrets, tokens, or real client/matter/document data appear in changed files (references and boolean attestations only). changed-file-scope cleanly separates active pack refs from unrelated dirty files with none omitted, and the readiness auth preflight passed (exit 0, valid JSON, 1434-byte stdout), satisfying CP178+ evidence hardening for this gate. No P0/P1/P2 issues found. One P3 provenance note: this verdict rests on static Read/Grep/Glob inspection; the command-evidence exit codes (npm test/validate/build/etc.) are pack-asserted and executed by the closeout harness, not re-run by the reviewer. The pack is sound to close at the descriptor level; production_ready/runtime_ready gates remain unweakened.

findings.title: Verdict based on static read-only inspection; validation-matrix exit codes are pack-asserted, not reviewer-re-executed
findings.details: command-evidence.json reports exit_code 0 for the full pre-closeout validation matrix (node --check on the changed sources, npm run closeout-pack-plan:validate, closeout-pack:validate, rp22:external-integrations-i:validate, rp22:validate, weighted:validate, spec:requirements:validate, fullplan:validate, validate, goal:closeout:validate, rp00:control-plane:validate, git diff --check, npm test, npm run build). As a read-only reviewer limited to Read/Grep/Glob (no Bash), I independently confirmed these results are consistent with the static repository state -- registry.js/service.js/validators.js are internally coherent, the CP670 model tests assert coverage and the no-runtime/no-write descriptor boundaries, and the contract-projection generator output matches both committed contract files (generated_from_pack_id and current_pack.pack_id = CP00-670 in contracts/external-integrations-i-contract.json and contracts/integrations-core-contract.json) -- but I did not and cannot re-execute the commands. The exit codes are therefore pack-asserted evidence, independently executed by the closeout harness rather than verified by this review.
findings.recommendation: Before adjudication writes production_ready, ensure the closeout harness's own live execution of the validation matrix (not merely the recorded exit codes in command-evidence.json) gates promotion. No code or artifact change is required; this is a provenance disclosure for the audit trail.
findings.post_review_disposition: Acknowledged -- informational provenance disclosure; does not block CP00-670 pack closeout.
